notes: Cross-service shared-library culprit (version skew). The true cause is the shared schema module commit ab35071b, which renames the metric-envelope field 'meta' -> 'metadata' (json tag and the required-fields list in envelope_gen.go). The producer (pipeline-transformer) bumps that shared module to v0.9.0 (commit 1f0d0d35) and deploys at 10:52, so it begins emitting envelopes keyed 'metadata'. metric-ingestor-1 still links the old schema and its strict decoder requires 'meta', so every envelope fails with 'missing required field meta' -> it stops flushing to olapdb -> the metric-ingestor-1-iq queue backs up and kafka-metric-ingestor reports rising ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage. metric-ingestor-1's OWN code did not change in a breaking way. Tempting wrong answers: (1) the innocent ingestor refactor 4e169b78 deployed to the loud failing service at 10:57 right at onset (bait: latest deploy, on the loud service, touches decode.go but only restructures decoder construction); (2) the pipeline-transformer go.mod bump 1f0d0d35 (the delivery vehicle, but the breaking change lives in the shared module it pulls in, not in the bump itself); (3) the aws-sdk-go-v2 bump 17f8bae4 on kafka-metric-ingestor near onset. Reading the diffs: only ab35071b actually renames the field that the decoder requires. Remediation: roll back the shared schema module (revert ab35071b) / the producer to the matching version.
